import json
import pandas as pd
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Load full data
tracks_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
with open(tracks_file_path, 'r') as f:
    tracks = json.load(f)

sales_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(sales_file_path, 'r') as f:
    sales = json.load(f)

# Create DataFrames
df_tracks = pd.DataFrame(tracks)
df_sales = pd.DataFrame(sales)

# Normalize functions for entity resolution
def normalize_title(title):
    if not title or title == 'None':
        return ''
    # Lowercase and remove extra whitespace
    title = str(title).lower().strip()
    # Remove common prefixes and suffixes that might vary
    title = re.sub(r'^(\d+-|[^a-zA-Z0-9]+)', '', title)  # Remove leading numbers and dashes
    title = re.sub(r'\s*\(.*?\)\s*$', '', title)  # Remove trailing parentheses
    title = re.sub(r'\s*-\s*.*$', '', title)  # Remove trailing dash and content
    return title.strip()

def normalize_artist(artist):
    if not artist or artist in ['None', '[unknown]', '   ', '']:
        return ''
    artist = str(artist).lower().strip()
    # Remove "none" and common prefixes
    if artist == 'none':
        return ''
    return artist

def normalize_album(album):
    if not album or album == 'None':
        return ''
    return str(album).lower().strip()

def normalize_year(year):
    if not year or year == 'None':
        return None
    year = str(year).strip()
    # Handle various year formats
    if year.startswith("'") and len(year) == 3:  # '05, '96
        year = '19' + year[1:] if int(year[1:]) >= 50 else '20' + year[1:]
    elif len(year) == 2:  # 75, 05, 96
        if year.isdigit():
            year_num = int(year)
            year = '19' + year if year_num >= 50 else '20' + year
    elif len(year) == 4 and year.isdigit():
        return int(year)
    try:
        return int(year)
    except:
        return None

# Apply normalization
df_tracks['norm_title'] = df_tracks['title'].apply(normalize_title)
df_tracks['norm_artist'] = df_tracks['artist'].apply(normalize_artist)
df_tracks['norm_album'] = df_tracks['album'].apply(normalize_album)
df_tracks['norm_year'] = df_tracks['year'].apply(normalize_year)

# Remove rows with no identifying information
df_tracks_filtered = df_tracks[
    (df_tracks['norm_title'] != '') | 
    (df_tracks['norm_artist'] != '') | 
    (df_tracks['norm_album'] != '')
].copy()

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'original_tracks': len(df_tracks),
    'filtered_tracks': len(df_tracks_filtered),
    'sales_records': len(df_sales),
    'sample_normalized': df_tracks_filtered[['track_id', 'title', 'norm_title', 'artist', 'norm_artist']].head(10).to_dict('records')
}))

df_sales['total_units'] = pd.to_numeric(df_sales['total_units'])

    """Clean and normalize text for matching"""
    if not text or text in ['None', '[unknown]', '   ', '', 'null']:
        return ''
    text = str(text).lower().strip()

    text = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s]', '', text)
    return text.strip()

def extract_title_parts(title):
    """Extract main title parts, removing prefixes, suffixes, and version info"""
    if not title or title == 'None':
        return ''
    title = str(title).lower().strip()
    # Remove leading track numbers (001-, 007-, etc.)
    title = re.sub(r'^\d+-\s*', '', title)
    # Remove content in parentheses at the end
    title = re.sub(r'\s*\([^)]*\)\s*$', '', title)
    # Remove content in brackets at the end
    title = re.sub(r'\s*\[[^\]]*\]\s*$', '', title)
    # Remove common suffixes
    title = re.sub(r'\s*-\s*.*$', '', title)
    # Clean

    title = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s]', '', title)
    return title.strip()

    if not year or year == 'None':

    year = str(year).strip()

    if match:
        return int(match.group(1))
    # Handle 2-digit years
    match = re.search(r'(\d{2})', year)
    if match:
        year_num = int(match.group(1))
        return 1900 + year_num if year_num >= 50 else 2000 + year_num
    return None

import json
import pandas as pd
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Load full data
tracks_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
with open(tracks_file_path, 'r') as f:
    tracks = json.load(f)

sales_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(sales_file_path, 'r') as f:
    sales = json.load(f)

# Create DataFrames
df_tracks = pd.DataFrame(tracks)
df_sales = pd.DataFrame(sales)

# Convert revenue and units to numeric
df_sales['total_revenue'] = pd.to_numeric(df_sales['total_revenue'])
df_sales['total_units'] = pd.to_numeric(df_sales['total_units'])

# Enhanced normalization functions
def clean_text(text):
    if not text or text in ['None', '[unknown]', '   ', '']:
        return ''
    text = str(text).lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s]', '', text)
    return text.strip()

def extract_title(title):
    if not title or title == 'None':
        return ''
    title = str(title).lower().strip()
    title = re.sub(r'^\d+-\s*', '', title)
    title = re.sub(r'\s*\([^)]*\)\s*$', '', title)
    title = re.sub(r'\s*\[[^\]]*\]\s*$', '', title)
    title = re.sub(r'\s*-\s*.*$', '', title)
    title = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', title)
    title = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s]', '', title)
    return title.strip()

def normalize_year(year):
    if not year or year == 'None':
        return None
    year = str(year).strip()
    match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', year)
    if match:
        return int(match.group(1))
    match = re.search(r'(\d{2})', year)
    if match:
        year_num = int(match.group(1))
        return 1900 + year_num if year_num >= 50 else 2000 + year_num
    return None

# Apply normalization
df_tracks['clean_title'] = df_tracks['title'].apply(lambda x: extract_title(str(x)))
df_tracks['clean_artist'] = df_tracks['artist'].apply(lambda x: clean_text(str(x)))
df_tracks['clean_album'] = df_tracks['album'].apply(lambda x: clean_text(str(x)))
df_tracks['norm_year'] = df_tracks['year'].apply(normalize_year)

# Filter out completely empty records
df_tracks_filtered = df_tracks[
    (df_tracks['clean_title'] != '') | 
    (df_tracks['clean_artist'] != '')
].copy()

# Merge with sales data to get only tracks with sales
df_sales_info = df_tracks_filtered.merge(df_sales, on='track_id', how='inner')

# Group by normalized attributes (entity resolution)
# Use multiple grouping strategies to catch different variations
grouped = df_sales_info.groupby(['clean_title', 'clean_artist', 'clean_album', 'norm_year'])

# Sum revenues and units for each group
song_revenues = grouped.agg({
    'total_revenue': 'sum',
    'total_units': 'sum',
    'track_id': 'count'  # Count how many track_ids represent this song
}).reset_index()

song_revenues = song_revenues.rename(columns={'track_id': 'track_count'})
song_revenues = song_revenues.sort_values('total_revenue', ascending=False)

# Get the top result
top_song = song_revenues.iloc[0]

# Get actual track info for this song (the one with highest individual revenue)
song_tracks = df_sales_info[
    (df_sales_info['clean_title'] == top_song['clean_title']) &
    (df_sales_info['clean_artist'] == top_song['clean_artist']) &
    (df_sales_info['clean_album'] == top_song['clean_album'])
]

# Get the representative track (the one with highest individual revenue)
representative_track = song_tracks.loc[song_tracks['total_revenue'].idxmax()]

result = {
    'song_title': representative_track['title'],
    'artist': representative_track['artist'] if representative_track['artist'] != 'None' else 'Unknown',
    'album': representative_track['album'] if representative_track['album'] != 'None' else 'Unknown',
    'year': representative_track['year'] if representative_track['year'] != 'None' else 'Unknown',
    'total_revenue': float(top_song['total_revenue']),
    'total_units': float(top_song['total_units']),
    'track_variants': int(top_song['track_count']),
    'normalized_title': top_song['clean_title'],
    'normalized_artist': top_song['clean_artist']
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))
